title: Therapeutic Medical Scientist (MSL) - Central Italy
location: Italy
description:
Pharma Point on behalf of Cytokinetics is looking for a Therapeutic Medical Scientist (MSL) to join the Medical Affairs team in Italy.

Territory: Central Italy

Cytokinetics is a late-stage, specialty cardiovascular biopharmaceutical company focused on discovering, developing and commercializing first-in-class muscle activators and next-in-class muscle inhibitors for diseases in which cardiac muscle performance is compromised.

As a leader in muscle biology, the company is advancing small molecule therapies designed to directly impact myocardial function and contractility.

The Role

Reporting to the Senior Medical Director Italy, the TMS will act as a key field-based scientific expert, engaging with KOLs and healthcare professionals, and supporting the execution of the medical strategy across the cardiovascular area.

Key Responsibilities
• Develop and maintain scientific relationships with KOLs and HCPs at national and regional level
• Lead scientific exchange and identify unmet medical needs within cardiovascular disease (focus on HCM)
• Act as scientific expert at congresses, investigator meetings, and scientific events
• Maintain deep knowledge of disease area, clinical data, guidelines, and competitive landscape
• Support clinical development activities, including interaction with investigators and research projects
• Collaborate cross-functionally with Medical Affairs, Clinical, and Commercial teams
• Contribute to medical education, publications, training activities, and scientific programs
• Provide medical insights to support account planning and field strategy
• Ensure timely handling of medical information requests and PV-related activities
• Prepare field medical plans, congress reports, and competitive intelligence insights
• Ensure accurate documentation of activities in company systems

For Senior level:
• Mentor junior MSLs and provide scientific training internally
• Contribute to strategic planning and execution of regional medical plans
• Lead or support congress planning and cross-functional initiatives

Requirements
• Scientific degree (PhD, MD, PharmD or equivalent)
• Previous experience as MSL (2–5+ years for Senior level)
• Experience in cardiovascular area preferred (HCM or heart failure a plus)
• Strong scientific communication and stakeholder engagement skills
• Knowledge of compliance (AIFA / Farmindustria / ABPI)
• Fluent English
• Willingness to travel (30–50%)

title: Medical Scientific Liaison - Southern Italy (based in Rome/Naples)
location: Rome, Metropolitan City of Rome Capital, Italy
description:
Do you want to make a real difference in the lives of people living with rare diseases? Do you have experience as a Medical Science Liaison (MSL) – and are you ready to bring your skills to a dynamic and purpose-driven company? If so, this is your chance to join Ascendis Pharma Italy.

Ascendis Pharma is a global biopharmaceutical company committed to making a meaningful difference in patients’ lives. Guided by our core values of Patients, Science, and Passion, we are applying our innovative TransCon® technology platform to fulfill our mission of developing new therapies that demonstrate best-in-class potential to address unmet medical needs. At our headquarters in Hellerup, Denmark, research facilities in Heidelberg, Germany, and additional offices across Europe and the United States, we are advancing programs in our Endocrinology Rare Disease and Oncology portfolios. We also collaborate with partners on the development of TransCon-based products in other therapeutic areas and markets.

We are seeking a passionate and committed individual to join our Italian team where you will play a crucial role in identifying and developing relationships with key opinion leaders (KOLs), supporting scientific collaborations with scientific societies and clinical experts, and educating healthcare providers about the disease states and products in the Ascendis Pharma pipeline.

This is an exciting opportunity to become part of a fast-paced environment, collaborate with cross-functional, global teams working together to achieve extraordinary results.

You will report directly to the Medical Affairs Director Italy, who is based in Milan. Your role will be remote/field-based.
Your key responsibilities
• Serve as the field-based external facing representative of Ascendis Pharma Medical Affairs.
• Create awareness of Ascendis Pharma in the scientific community and communicate the potential of TransCon® technology to transform the practice of endocrinology and other relevant therapeutic areas (TAs).
• Maintain high level of medical expertise to support the portfolio/pipeline and relevant disease states (e.g., bone and growth).
• Identify Key Opinion Leaders (KOLs) and key organisations (e.g. professional associations, patient advisory groups) for disease states within a geography and develop and maintain a KOL engagement plan that foster collaborative relationships.
• Plan and deliver medical/scientific presentations to both internal and external stakeholders, provide scientific support at medical congresses to include scientific exchange at the medical affairs booth, and internal or external meetings, as well as internal onboarding and ongoing medical training for commercial and medical colleagues.
• Capture external insights about the disease state, Ascendis portfolio and pipeline, and competitors and communicate to internal stakeholders in a timely and effective manner.
• Build strong relationships and cross-functional collaboration with internal colleagues at local as well as above country level (e.g., Clinical Development, HEOR, Market Access, Marketing, and Sales) by providing medical expertise and support as needed.
Qualifications and Skills

You hold a relevant academic degree – preferably a PhD, PharmD, M.Sc., DVM, or MD degree from the Health Science Field – and 2+ years of pharmaceutical experience as a MSL or a similar role.

you have:
• Experience within Pediatric endocrinology, endocrinology, or Rare Diseases
• Strong oral and written communication skills with a high capability to understand and effectively communicate scientific and clinical information in virtual and live settings as required
Key competencies

You are a strong team player, empathetic, and have a can-do attitude. You possess an entrepreneurial mindset and can thrive in an informal, open environment where innovation and change are key.

To succeed in this role, we also expect you to be a passionate and out-going people person who finds excitement in the challenge of being one of the first employees in a new affiliate.
Apply now

Applications will be evaluated when received, so please apply as soon as possible.

All applications must be submitted in English and are treated confidentially.

If you are passionate about contributing to the field of rare diseases and eager to make a difference at Ascendis Pharma, we encourage you to apply for this exciting opportunity. Join us in our mission to advance therapeutic innovations and improve patient lives worldwide.
#J-18808-Ljbffr

title: Msl Centro Italia (Cardiometabolic)
location: Gallicano nel Lazio, Metropolitan City of Rome Capital, Italy
description:
Descrizione

We are looking for a MSL (Cardiometabolic Area) - Center of Italy, on behalf of Sobi, a global biopharma company unlocking the potential of breakthrough innovations, transforming everyday life for people living with rare diseases.

The Medical Science Liaison (MSL) is a field‑based scientific expert responsible for engaging healthcare professionals, clinical experts, and key external stakeholders in support of the agreed medical strategy for Tryngolza, an innovative therapy for Familial Chylomicronemia Syndrome (FCS), with future potential in Severe Hypertriglyceridemia (SHT).

In this role, you will drive scientific exchange, support clinical and evidence‑generation initiatives, gather actionable insights, and partner cross‑functionally to ensure consistent, high-quality scientific standards. The position plays a critical part in enhancing understanding of rare lipid disorders, supporting patient pathways, and enabling informed, ethical decision‑making.
Executes field medical activities in accordance with Medical Excellence standards, including qualitative insights capture, engagement planning discipline, and agreed performance measures.

External Scientific Engagement
• Serve as the primary field‑based medical and scientific expert for Tryngolza within the sHTG community.
• Build and maintain strong, trusted relationships with:
• Key opinion leaders (KOLs)
• Healthcare professionals
• Academic researchers
• Conduct non‑promotional scientific discussions on disease pathways, clinical evidence, patient management, and emerging data, in line with approved medical content.
• Ensure high‑quality, balanced scientific exchange and clear communication of relevant information.
• Develop and maintain stakeholder engagement plans aligned to Medical Excellence guidance (segmentation, objectives, scientific exchange plans, and documentation)

Insight Generation & Strategic Contribution
• Gather and communicate actionable insights from HCP interactions, scientific meetings, and stakeholder activities.
• Share insights with Medical Affairs colleagues to shape:
• Medical strategy
• Evidence‑generation priorities
• Stakeholder engagement plans
• Educational programs
• Key account plans in a cross-functional manner
• Identify and communicate emerging gaps in diagnosis, treatment access, and unmet needs.
• Contribute to periodic insight reviews (e.G., monthly/quarterly) to support cross-country visibility and strategic decision-making

Annuncio valido fino a: 31-May-2026

Azienda

global biopharma company

Requisiti

- Advanced life sciences degree required (e.G., PharmD, PhD, MD, MSc or equivalent).- Prior experience as an MSL or in a similar customer‑facing medical role preferred.- Experience in specialty care, metabolic disorders, or highly scientific therapy areas is strongly beneficial.- Proven experience engaging with HCPs, building external relationships, and driving meaningful scientific exchange.- Strong scientific understanding and ability to explain complex data clearly.- Excellent communication, presentation and interpersonal skills.- Ability to build credibility and trust with clinical and scientific experts.- Strong analytical and insight‑generation capability.- Comfort working autonomously in the field, with strong organizational skills.- Ability to collaborate effectively with internal cross‑functional teams.- High ethical standards and commitment to unbiased scientific engagement.- Flexibility to travel for external meetings, site visits, and medical events.- Good knowledge of English.

Mettiamo a disposizione manager e consulenti altamente qualificati, con esperienza in tutti i principali settori industriali. Jeffersonwells è parte del Gruppo Manpower e riconosciuta tra le "World's most ethical companies."

title: Medical Scientific Liaison (MSL) - Central Italy
location: Rome, Metropolitan City of Rome Capital, Italy
description:
Apply now »

Medical Scientific Liaison (MSL) - Central Italy

Date: Mar 6, 2026

Location: Rome, Italy, 00186

Company: Teva Pharmaceuticals

Job Id: 66701

We Are Teva

We’re Teva, a leading innovative biopharmaceutical company, enabled by a world-class generics business. Whether it’s innovating in the fields of neuroscience and immunology or delivering high-quality medicine worldwide, we’re dedicated to addressing patients’ needs now and in the future. Here, you will be part of a high-performing, inclusive culture that values fresh thinking and collaboration. You'll have the room to grow, the flexibility to balance life with work, and the opportunity to better health worldwide, together.

Our Team, Your Impact

As a Medical Scientific Liaison (MSL) you will maximize Company’s value propositions through medical and scientific projects, services and expertise, developing long-term partnership with medical community for benefit of patients.

We want this person to be based/live in Rome or the sorrounding area.

You will be covering the following regions: Lazio, Umbria, Marche, Abruzzi, Molise and Sardegna.

How You’ll Spend Your Day

In line with Teva Italia strategy, therapeutic area and products of responsibility, the MSL will be responsible for:
• Clinical and medical-scientific education for both internal and external stakeholders (KOLs, general and junior physicians, hospital pharmacists, nurses, internal Teva stakeholders...)
• Stakeholders mapping and management (i.e.KOLs)
• Support to clinical and post-marketing Teva sponsored studies (CSS), Investigator sponsored (ISS) and Collaborative research (CRS) studies
• Support to marketing and commercial activities
• Information Management /Medical Insights & Scientific Steatment
• Ensure medical/legal excellence and compliance
• Ensure that local Medical Affairs activities follow National and Local regulations
• Represent Company Medical department in the territory, participating to local, regional, National and International congress and producing qualitative reports regarding the more relevant communications

Your Skills and Experience

Background:
• Degree in Medicine, PharmD, Medical / Pharmaceutical Biotecnology or a similar Healthcare degree
• At least 5 years of experience as MSL
• Knowledge of the research process and statistical analysis to evaluate the effectiveness of clinical programs
• Working knowledge of the Healthcare System and of the research procedures relevant to the pharmaceutical industry
• Fluent English (part of the interview process will be conducted in English, so a fluent oral level of English is required)

Skills:
• Customer orientation
• Strategic vision
• Excellence in execution
• Collaboration and influencing

How We’ll Take Care of You

At Teva, we believe that well-being begins with people. We offer health insurance, a retirement fund, meal plans, company laptops and iPhones.

And for your professional growth, there's Twist: our dedicated development platform. Here you'll find training, temporary projects, and internal growth opportunities.

At Teva, you can explore, evolve, and build your own path, with a culture that puts your well-being and goals at the center.

Reports To

Sr Dir Medical Department Teva Italia & South-East Hub

Already Working @TEVA?

Make sure to apply through our internal career site on Twist—your one-stop shop for career development

Teva’s Equal Employment Opportunity Commitment

Teva Pharmaceuticals is committed to equal opportunity in employment. It is Teva's global policy that equal employment opportunity be provided without regard to age, race, creed, color, religion, sex, disability, pregnancy, medical condition, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, ancestry, veteran status, national or ethnic origin or any other legally recognized status entitled to protection under applicable laws. We are committed to a diverse and inclusive workplace for all. If you are contacted for a job opportunity, please advise us of any accommodations needed to support you throughout the recruitment and selection process. All accommodation information provided will be treated as confidential and used only for the purpose of providing an accessible candidate experience.

Apply now »

title: Senior Medical Science Liaison
location: Rome, Metropolitan City of Rome Capital, Italy
description:
We are looking for highly motivated Medical Science Liaisons with a proven track record of successfully engaging neurology healthcare professionals, key opinion leaders (KOLs), and organizations in order to support trial enrollment and success of our Phase 2 clinical trial.

Qualifiche, competenze e l'esperienza necessaria per questo ruolo sono disponibili nella descrizione completa riportata di seguito.

The Medical Science Liaison is responsible for cultivating current and future thought leaders in neurology in private practices, community and academic settings within their assigned region/geography. The incumbents’ clinical practice insights and science knowledge will be utilized to deliver non-promotional scientific, educational, and research data and activities. The position will leverage demonstrated scientific leadership in neurodegenerative disease, preferably within Parkinson’s Disease and serve as a key representative of our client with external stakeholders

Principal Accountabilities
Develop and execute strategic enrollment plans in collaboration with clinical operations to optimize site performance and accelerate patient recruitment.
Partner closely with investigators and sites to identify recruitment barriers and implement targeted solutions to improve enrolment.
Identify and develop relationships with KOLs, physicians, and advocacy groups, based on balanced scientific exchange.
Form strategic partnerships with key academic medical centers to advance development efforts in therapeutic areas pertinent to our client's pipeline.
With assistance from the clinical team, develop and author publications and abstracts; coordinate efforts with external collaborators.
Build a strong network of pertinent and relevant experts to obtain relevant advice, expert opinions, and collaborations
Attend regional, national and international medical conferences to enhance awareness of the clients clinical trials.
Represent the client at scientific/medical symposia, congresses, patient advocacy events and industry councils as requested
Deliver clear, concise, and relevant clinical/scientific presentations in a variety of settings, including but not limited to internal meetings, advisory boards, investigator meetings, patient advocacy sessions, academic/group practice/individual discussions, and other venues.
Identify clinical insights and knowledge gaps and be able to articulate the impact of the clients scientific approach
Organize and run KOL Advisory Board and engage KOLs in activities.
Support development and management of conference presentations and other data dissemination activities.

Qualifications

An understanding of the overall drug development process typically achieved through an advanced degree in medical science (e.g. MD, PhD, PharmD, NP, or PA) and 7 or more years of work experience. Multiple years of experience in neurological disorders, preferably Parkinson’s disease, and/or immunology are differentiators among candidates.
Current relationships in the Movement Disorders community and KOLs will also be a strong differentiator.
Working knowledge of GCP, ICH guidelines and FDA regulations.
Excellent time management skills with the ability to prioritize deadlines and multitask.
Self-starter with a demonstrated ability to work in collaboration and cooperation with members of a multidisciplinary team.
Team player with ability to motivate and influence cross-functional team members.
Ability to handle multiple projects simultaneously, communicate clearly and effectively, and build collaborative relationships.
Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, entrepreneurial environment, working collaboratively as well as independently, under broad strategic guidance. xrdbqlu
Verbal and written communication skills to the level of effectively producing and delivering compelling and insightful presentations to diverse audiences.

title: Medical science liaison/msl (respiratorio)
location: Rome, Metropolitan City of Rome Capital, Italy
description:
Siamo alla ricerca di un/a Medical Science Liaison con esperienza specifica sul target RESPIRATORIO. NON SARANNO PRESE IN CONSIDERAZIONE CANDIDATURE PROVENIENTI DA ALTRI TARGET.Ricerchiamo la risorsa per una prestigiosa realtà operante nel settore chimico farmaceutico, area respiratoria. La figura sarà responsabile di stabilire e mantenere relazioni strategiche con esperti del settore sanitario, fornendo supporto scientifico e garantendo una comunicazione efficace tra l'azienda e la comunità medico-scientifica.Responsabilità principali:
• Costruire e mantenere relazioni professionali con opinion leader, specialisti e stakeholder del settore sanitario.
• Fornire informazioni scientifiche aggiornate e supporto tecnico ai professionisti sanitari.
• Collaborare con il team interno per lo sviluppo di strategie e materiali scientifici.
• Partecipare a congressi, eventi e seminari per rappresentare l'azienda e promuovere le soluzioni proposte.
• Monitorare e analizzare le tendenze del mercato e gli sviluppi scientifici rilevanti per il settore.Requisiti richiesti:
• Esperienza di almeno 4/5 anni nel ruolo di MSL, sultarget Respiratorio (terapia inalatoria)
In alternativa valutiamo anche chi ha ricoperto il ruolo di Medical Affairs sempre però nel RESPIRATORIO.
• Laurea in discipline scientifiche (es. Medicina, Farmacia, Biologia, Biotecnologie o similari).
• Conoscenza approfondita del settore sanitario e delle normative di riferimento.
• Eccellenti capacità comunicative e relazionali.
• Ottima conoscenza della lingua inglese, sia scritta che parlata.
• Disponibilità a spostamenti sul Centro ItaliaSi offre un'opportunità stimolante in un contesto innovativo e dinamico, con possibilità di crescita professionale. L'inizio del contratto è previsto per Febbraio compatibilmente con eventuale periodo di preavviso.
Contratto di 12 mesi con Adecco ed eventuale rinnovo/inserimentoRal proposta in base all'esperienza maturata + tickets da 7€Ccnl CommercioAuto aziendale ad uso promiscuo, telefono, PC e tablet, Proiettore.Domicilio nel LazioLa risorsa gestirà le seguenti zone:Lazio e Toscana principalmentema anche Abruzzo, Marche e Umbria.Se sei interessato/a a questa posizione e desideri far parte di un ambiente altamente professionale, invia la tua candidatura. La ricerca è rivolta a persone di ogni genere e provenienza.

title: MEDICAL SCIENTIFIC LIAISON - Lazio, Umbria, Toscana e isole
location: Rome, Metropolitan City of Rome Capital, Italy
description:
Tailor&Fox, società di HeadHunting, sta supportando per conto di importante realtà farmaceutica internazionale operante in ambito oncologico la ricerca della seguente figura professionale:

MEDICAL SCIENTIFIC LIAISON

Riportando al Medical Manager, la figura sarà responsabile dello sviluppo e del consolidamento delle relazioni scientifiche con la comunità medica, favorendo una corretta comprensione del portfolio prodotti e supportando un utilizzo appropriato nelle indicazioni approvate, nel pieno rispetto delle normative vigenti e procedure interne. Il ruolo prevede un’attività prevalentemente sul campo (circa 80%).

Principali responsabilità:
• Supportare le attività di pre-lancio e lancio di nuovi prodotti in ambito oncologico.
• Mappare e segmentare il territorio assegnato, identificando account e clinici a maggiore potenziale scientifico.
• Costruire e mantenere relazioni con Key Opinion Leader, sviluppando opportunità scientifiche (pubblicazioni, presentazioni, meeting locali e regionali).
• Organizzare incontri scientifici, sessioni dipartimentali e Advisory Board.
• Supportare programmi di early access e il follow-up di studi clinici sponsorizzati e IIS approvati.
• Fornire formazione scientifica continua al team sul territorio.
• Raccogliere e condividere insight scientifici con il dipartimento medico internazionale.
• Gestire le richieste di informazioni mediche tramite gli strumenti aziendali dedicati.
• Partecipare a congressi nazionali e, se richiesto, internazionali come rappresentante medico.

Requisiti:
• Laurea in discipline scientifiche (ai sensi della L. 219/2006)
• Solida preparazione scientifica
• Capacità di analisi, interpretazione e presentazione di dati scientifici
• Ottima conoscenza della lingua inglese
• Disponibilità a frequenti trasferte
• Esperienza in oncologia considerata un plus

Richiesta residenza : Lazio.
Zone di competenza : Lazio, Umbria, Toscana Sicilia e Sardegna.

La ricerca è rivolta ad entrambi i sessi, secondo UNI/PdR 125:2022

title: QA Supervisor
location: Badia Polesine, VEN, IT
description:
**Requistion ID**: 82736

  

Dentsply Sirona is the world’s largest manufacturer of professional dental products and technologies, with a 130\-year history of innovation and service to the dental industry and patients worldwide. Dentsply Sirona develops, manufactures, and markets a comprehensive solutions offering including dental and oral health products as well as other consumable medical devices under a strong portfolio of world class brands. Dentsply Sirona’s products provide innovative, high\-quality and effective solutions to advance patient care and deliver better and safer dentistry. Dentsply Sirona’s global headquarters is located in Charlotte, North Carolina, USA. The company’s shares are listed in the United States on NASDAQ under the symbol XRAY.

 **Bringing out the best in people**

  



As advanced as dentistry is today, we are dedicated to making it even better. Our people have a passion for innovation and are committed to applying it to improve dental care. We live and breathe high performance, working as one global team, bringing out the best in each other for the benefit of dental patients, and the professionals who serve them. If you want to grow and develop as a part of a team that is shaping an industry, then we’re looking for the best to join us.

  



Working at Dentsply Sirona you are able to:

  



**Develop faster** \- with our commitment to the best professional development.

  



**Perform better** \- as part of a high\-performance, empowering culture.

  



**Shape an industry** \- with a market leader that continues to drive innovation.

  



**Make a difference** \-by helping improve oral health worldwide.

 **For our production site in Badia Polesine, we are looking for a:**

 **QA Supervisor**

  

The position, reporting to the Sr. QA Manager, will be responsible for ensuring that inspection activities on materials (incoming, in‑process, and finished product) are carried out in accordance with established requirements.

 **Responsibilities**

* Ensure that internal audit processes, calibration activities, complaint investigations, non‑conformity investigations, and CAPA are carried out in accordance with company standards.
* Ensure that quality personnel are properly trained and informed about applicable procedures.
* Knowledge of ISO 13485, MDR, and other standards related to the medical device sector.
* Ability to balance individual and team responsibilities and contribute to a positive work environment.
* Familiarity with sampling plans (ANSI, AQL, etc.).
* Qualification as an internal auditor.
* Knowledge of good laboratory practices.

 **Skills**

* Experience in quality control for medical devices or similar sectors.
* Knowledge of the Microsoft Office suite.
* Preferably able to interpret mechanical drawings.
* Basic knowledge of using calipers, microscopes, digital scales, optical comparators.
* Understanding of ERP system functionality (SAP/AX).
* Team‑oriented mindset.
* Good written and verbal communication skills.
* Fluent knowledge of Italian.
* Knowledge of English (B2 level is sufficient).
* Orderliness and accuracy in data recording and proper archiving.
* Alignment with corporate values: Respect (for company standards, vision, colleagues and customers), Responsibility, Collaboration, Reliability, and Agility (continuous improvement).

  

Dentsply Sirona is an Equal Opportunity/ Affirmative Action employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, sexual orientation, disability, or protected Veteran status. We appreciate your interest in Dentsply Sirona.


If you need assistance with completing the online application due to a disability, please send an accommodation request to careers@dentsplysirona.com. Please be sure to include “Accommodation Request” in the subject.

title: Automation Engineer
location: Badia Polesine, VEN, IT
description:
**Requistion ID**: 82740

  

Dentsply Sirona is the world’s largest manufacturer of professional dental products and technologies, with a 130\-year history of innovation and service to the dental industry and patients worldwide. Dentsply Sirona develops, manufactures, and markets a comprehensive solutions offering including dental and oral health products as well as other consumable medical devices under a strong portfolio of world class brands. Dentsply Sirona’s products provide innovative, high\-quality and effective solutions to advance patient care and deliver better and safer dentistry. Dentsply Sirona’s global headquarters is located in Charlotte, North Carolina, USA. The company’s shares are listed in the United States on NASDAQ under the symbol XRAY.

 **Bringing out the best in people**

  



As advanced as dentistry is today, we are dedicated to making it even better. Our people have a passion for innovation and are committed to applying it to improve dental care. We live and breathe high performance, working as one global team, bringing out the best in each other for the benefit of dental patients, and the professionals who serve them. If you want to grow and develop as a part of a team that is shaping an industry, then we’re looking for the best to join us.

  



Working at Dentsply Sirona you are able to:

  



**Develop faster** \- with our commitment to the best professional development.

  



**Perform better** \- as part of a high\-performance, empowering culture.

  



**Shape an industry** \- with a market leader that continues to drive innovation.

  



**Make a difference** \-by helping improve oral health worldwide.

 **For our production site in Badia Polesine, we are looking for an:**

 **Automation Engineer**

  

The position will be responsible for the design, development, and implementation of industrial automation solutions, handling programming, testing, commissioning, process optimization, and technical support to ensure efficiency, quality, and reliability of the systems.

 **Responsibilities**

* Responsible for the design and development of automation systems (PLC, HMI, SCADA, robotics), defining process requirements and technical specifications.
* Responsible for diagnosing complex faults on PLCs, industrial networks, sensors, and actuators.
* Manages commissioning and testing of systems, organizing Factory Acceptance Tests (FAT) and Site Acceptance Tests (SAT), overseeing start‑up, calibration, and optimization of control logic.
* Supports the maintenance team in ensuring correct operation and efficiency of systems in compliance with current Safety, Environmental, and Quality procedures.
* Supports the engineering team with software updates, hardware upgrades, and troubleshooting during start‑ups, restarts, and normal plant operations.
* Defines process KPIs and performs data analysis to improve efficiency, quality, and scrap reduction (Lean approach).
* Coordinates suppliers and system integrators during field activities and ensures that all required technical documentation is prepared and archived (diagrams, manuals, declarations of conformity).
* Supports the QAS (Quality, Environment \& Safety Manager) in managing interactions with regulatory and certification bodies, and in preparing and reviewing Validation protocols within Operations.
* Respects company standards, corporate vision, colleagues, and customers.

 **Skills**

* Education: Degree in Automation Engineering, Mechatronics, Electronics, or equivalent.
* Fluent knowledge of Italian and technical English.
* Experience with MES integration and at least 3 years in a similar role.
* Knowledge of industry standards and regulations (ISO, CE, Machinery Directive).
* Technical drawing skills (reading P\&ID, electrical diagrams, automation layouts).
* Knowledge of industrial networks (PROFINET, EtherCAT, Ethernet/IP, Modbus).
* Industrial robotics experience (Fanuc, Epson, ABB, KUKA, Yaskawa).
* Knowledge of major SCADA/HMI systems (WinCC, FactoryTalk, Movicon, Ignition).
* Basic PLC programming skills (Siemens, Rockwell, Schneider, Omron or others depending on context).
* Experience with automation system design software (Studio 5000, FactoryTalk).
* Alignment with company values: Respect (for company standards, vision, colleagues and customers), Responsibility, Collaboration, Reliability, and Agility (continuous improvement).
* Flexibility: ability to adapt to context and support colleagues and supervisors.
* Participation in continuous improvement initiatives.
* Excellent knowledge of Microsoft Office tools.
* Strong organizational skills, multitasking ability, and autonomy.
* Precision, proactivity, and punctuality in meeting deadlines.

  

Dentsply Sirona is an Equal Opportunity/ Affirmative Action employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, sexual orientation, disability, or protected Veteran status. We appreciate your interest in Dentsply Sirona.


If you need assistance with completing the online application due to a disability, please send an accommodation request to careers@dentsplysirona.com. Please be sure to include “Accommodation Request” in the subject.

location: Badia Polesine, VEN, IT
description:
**Requistion ID**: 82735

  

Dentsply Sirona is the world’s largest manufacturer of professional dental products and technologies, with a 130\-year history of innovation and service to the dental industry and patients worldwide. Dentsply Sirona develops, manufactures, and markets a comprehensive solutions offering including dental and oral health products as well as other consumable medical devices under a strong portfolio of world class brands. Dentsply Sirona’s products provide innovative, high\-quality and effective solutions to advance patient care and deliver better and safer dentistry. Dentsply Sirona’s global headquarters is located in Charlotte, North Carolina, USA. The company’s shares are listed in the United States on NASDAQ under the symbol XRAY.

 **Bringing out the best in people**

  



As advanced as dentistry is today, we are dedicated to making it even better. Our people have a passion for innovation and are committed to applying it to improve dental care. We live and breathe high performance, working as one global team, bringing out the best in each other for the benefit of dental patients, and the professionals who serve them. If you want to grow and develop as a part of a team that is shaping an industry, then we’re looking for the best to join us.

  



Working at Dentsply Sirona you are able to:

  



**Develop faster** \- with our commitment to the best professional development.

  



**Perform better** \- as part of a high\-performance, empowering culture.

  



**Shape an industry** \- with a market leader that continues to drive innovation.

  



**Make a difference** \-by helping improve oral health worldwide.

  

For our production site in Badia Polesine rwe are looking for an:

 **HSE Specialist**

  



The position, reporting directly to the HSE Manager, will have the following responsibilities:

**Responsibilities**

* Based on the annual Health and Safety at Work inspection plan, ensures the execution of inspections to maintain the safety conditions of plants, equipment, and workplaces across all company areas, guaranteeing timely and effective interventions.
* Is responsible for corporate compliance with the PED directive.
* Is responsible for organizing safety training for company personnel, managing both onboarding for new hires and refresher training for current employees.
* Is responsible for compiling accident and near‑miss investigations, identifying appropriate corrective actions. Keeps the database updated for calculating accident, near‑miss, and severity indices.
* Manages documentation related to Article 26 of Legislative Decree 81/08 and promotes coordination and cooperation with external contractors.
* Maintains relations with the Occupational Health Physician and collaborates in planning and conducting workplace inspections and subsequently updating the Health Protocol when necessary.
* Collaborates with the team in managing the Safety Management System and renewing certifications such as ISO 45001 and ISO 14001\.
* Promotes awareness campaigns for personnel on HSE matters.
* Provides active support to various operational departments in drafting safety procedures.
* Supports the drafting, management, and updating of the Risk Assessment Document (DVR).
* Promotes, organizes, and conducts training and/or information sessions for personnel.
* Constantly verifies compliance with HSE regulations for all activities carried out within the plant.
* Participates in continuous improvement initiatives.

**Skills**

* University or postgraduate education related to health and safety.
* Fluent knowledge of Italian.
* Fluent/upper‑intermediate (B2\) level of English.
* Good/excellent knowledge of Microsoft Office.
* Ability to self‑organize and manage projects (time planning, milestones).
* Previous experience in industrial companies is appreciated but not strictly required.
* Alignment with corporate values: Respect (for company standards, corporate vision, colleagues and customers, processes and operational instructions), Responsibility, Collaboration, Reliability, and Agility (continuous improvement).
* Precision and attention to detail; ability to analyze and draft documents and regulations.
* Flexibility: ability to adapt one’s work to the context and to be supportive of colleagues and supervisors.
* Collaboration and teamwork with colleagues and managers within one’s own and other departments.

  

Dentsply Sirona is an Equal Opportunity/ Affirmative Action employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, sexual orientation, disability, or protected Veteran status. We appreciate your interest in Dentsply Sirona.


If you need assistance with completing the online application due to a disability, please send an accommodation request to careers@dentsplysirona.com. Please be sure to include “Accommodation Request” in the subject.

title: Field Service Technician (Medical Devices) - Italy
location: Bologna, EMI, IT
description:
Bologna, Italy \| Part time \| Field\-based \| R1536135**Job available in additional locations**### **Field Service Technician / Engineer (Medical Devices)**


**Hospitals \& Clinics** \| **Project‑Based (6–9 Months)**  

**Full‑Time or Part‑Time**
We are seeking a **Field Service Technician / Engineer** to support a **6–9 month project‑based assignment** focused on the **inspection, safety testing, and technical verification of medical devices** across hospitals and clinical environments.
This role is well‑suited for professionals seeking **contract, freelance, or project‑based work**, with flexibility for **full‑time or part‑time engagement** in an active, hands‑on clinical setting.**About the Role**
------------------


In this position, you will play a key role in ensuring that medical devices used in patient care meet **safety, performance, and compliance standards**. You will work directly within hospitals and clinics, collaborating closely with biomedical teams, clinicians, and facility stakeholders.**Key Responsibilities**
------------------------

* Conduct **on‑site inspections, checks, and technical verification** of medical devices in hospitals and clinics
* Ensure medical devices operate **safely and in compliance** with local regulations, applicable standards, and hospital procedures
* Perform or assist with **functional testing, safety testing, and preventive maintenance** activities
* Accurately **document findings and results** in maintenance or asset management systems
* Coordinate effectively with **clinical staff, biomedical departments, and hospital management**
* Escalate technical issues and **coordinate follow‑up actions or repairs** as required
* Work across **multiple clinical sites**, based on project requirements

**What You Bring**
------------------

* Qualification as an **Electronics, Mechatronics, Medical Device, or Biomedical Technician or Engineer**
* **At least 2 years of experience** working with medical devices, preferably in hospital or clinical environments
* Hands‑on experience in **medical device testing, inspection, servicing, or maintenance**
* Working knowledge of **medical device safety, quality, and compliance standards**
* Confident use of **MS Office** and digital documentation or reporting tools
* Ability to work **independently and reliably** in a project‑based environment
* Strong **communication skills** when engaging with both technical and non‑technical stakeholders
* **Good English communication skills**
* **Valid driving license**, required for multi‑site hospital work
* **Own vehicle**
* Willingness to **travel extensively within the country**

**Why Join This Project**
-------------------------

* **Short‑ to mid‑term engagement** with clearly defined scope and deliverables
* Hands‑on role with a **direct impact on patient safety and clinical operations**
* **Flexible working arrangements** (Full‑Time or Part‑Time)
* Exposure to a variety of **clinical environments, technologies, and medical devices**
* Well‑structured project support within a **professional and collaborative team**

description:
**Are you being referred to one of our roles by a connection in Alira Health?** If so, please apply using the referral link emailed to you.


Join our global team dedicated to innovation and initiative, where physical walls and different time zones don’t limit, but encourage, collaboration. Where all contributions and new ideas are explored with an open mind and work is driven by our shared values: be courageous, be accountable, be honest, be inclusive and elevate others.

**Job Description Summary**

**Job Description**

**ROLE**
--------


The Principal Statistical Programmer will support and lead statistical programming activities conducted on client clinical projects, including complex projects; producing high quality deliverables to agreed timelines and following relevant standard operating procedures. The principal statistical programmer will also support in the effective deployment of project resources and will support the maintenance of an efficient statistical programming department.

**KEY RESPONSABILITIES**
------------------------

* Lead statistical programming activities on clinical projects including complex projects
* Provide support in the identification of changes in project scope and potential risks to contractual delivery
* Act as support statistical programmer on clinical projects
* Specify, program and validate CDISC SDTM and ADaM datasets
* Specify, program and validate the statistical analysis results including analysis datasets, tables, listings and figures
* Produce and review CDISC documentation
* Create and validate randomization lists
* Ensure compliance to SOPs
* Report deviations, KPIs, customer complaints and managing CAPAs
* Provide supervision on tasks carried out by statistical programmers
* Provide guidance and mentoring to programming team members on procedures and technical standards
* Prepare and perform internal or external training on statistical programming techniques
* Identify, implement and develop new techniques, solutions, tools and products
* Identify areas for efficiency or process improvement
* Lead internal initiatives to improve department processes
* Communicate effectively with internal and external project teams
* Report issues to Project Manager/Associate Director in an appropriate timeframe
* Support Quality Assurance in internal and external audits
* Support the maintenance of an effective statistical programming department
* Conduct other statistical programming related activities as required
**DESIRED QUALIFICATION \& EXPERIENCE**
---------------------------------------

* BSc/MSc or equivalent with experience, preferably in Statistics, Computer Science, or Mathematics
* At least 5 years of experience in statistical programming
* Excellent SAS programming skills
* Knowledge of R language is a plus
* Very good knowledge of principles and techniques for clinical research

**TECHNICAL COMPETENCES \& SOFT SKILLS**
----------------------------------------

* Good English communication
* Good computer skills, with proficiency in use of Microsoft Word, Excel and PowerPoint
* Quality focused and results orientated
* Confident leader of teams
* Ability to effectively prioritize
* Trustworthy and strong team player
* Willingness to learn
* Ability to explain complex Statistical Programming concepts clearly
* Analytical thinking and problem solving skills
* Innovative thinker
**Languages**


English**Education**


Bachelor of Science (BS): Computer and Information Science, Bachelor of Science (BS): Mathematics, Bachelor of Science (BS): Statistics**Contract Type**


Regular
